does: This paper introduces ProAct, a proactive defense framework that protects large language models against multi-turn jailbreak attacks by intentionally generating misleading spurious responses. Rather than simply refusing harmful requests, the defense strategically provides false signals that deceive the attacker's iterative optimization loop into believing the model has been successfully jailbroken, causing the adversarial search to terminate prematurely. The method is evaluated across state-of-the-art LLMs, multiple jailbreaking frameworks, and safety benchmarks, demonstrating significant reductions in attack success rates by up to 94% while maintaining the model's utility on benign tasks.
connects: This work extends prior research on LLM safety alignment and adversarial robustness by moving beyond reactive defenses that simply block harmful outputs. It builds on foundational work in jailbreak detection and defense mechanisms while responding directly to the emergence of sophisticated multi-turn adversarial attacks that search iteratively for successful prompts. The approach combines insights from adversarial machine learning, where misleading gradients or signals have been used to disrupt optimization, with LLM-specific safety concerns. It also relates to broader research on manipulating attacker feedback and search strategies in adversarial settings.
enables: This framework opens new research directions in adversarial defense through deception and signal manipulation. Future work could explore more sophisticated spurious response generation strategies that adapt to different jailbreak methods, develop detection mechanisms to identify when models are under attack to trigger proactive defenses dynamically, or investigate adversarial robustness at deeper levels of LLM architectures. The approach may also inspire defenses for other AI systems vulnerable to iterative search attacks, and could lead to theoretical understanding of how feedback manipulation affects adversarial optimization in general. It raises important questions about defense mechanisms that intentionally deceive attackers and their broader implications for AI safety.
